title: elymus subfibrosus
intro: Elymus subfibrosus, also known as blue wildrye, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Elymus subfibrosus include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in some herbal remedies to promote relaxation and alleviate stress.
